name: Dhristadyumna
description: Dhristadyumna, also known as Yagnasena, is a prominent warrior in the epic Mahabharata. He was born of a sacrificial fire conducted by King Drupada of Panchala to avenge his humiliation at the hands of Drona. Destined to kill Drona, he was born fully armed, a testament to his martial prowess. He became the commander-in-chief of the Pandava army during the Kurukshetra War. Although a skilled warrior, his impulsive nature and lack of strategic depth sometimes led to difficulties. He played a crucial role in the war, leading numerous attacks and participating in key battles. His ultimate fate was to be killed by Ashwatthama in a sneak attack after the war, fulfilling the prophecy surrounding his birth. His birth, life, and death are intertwined with themes of revenge, destiny, and dharma.
subtitle: Born of fire, destined for war.
achievements: Born fully armed from sacrificial fire
Played a key role in Pandava victory

keyMyths.title: Birth from the Fire Altar
keyMyths.description: Dhristadyumna was born from a sacrificial fire conducted by King Drupada, along with his sister Draupadi. He emerged fully armed, signifying his destiny as a warrior and his purpose to kill Drona.

keyMyths.title: Commander-in-Chief
keyMyths.description: During the Kurukshetra War, Dhristadyumna was chosen as the commander-in-chief of the Pandava army. This showed the trust and respect the Pandavas had for his martial abilities and leadership.

keyMyths.title: Death by Ashwatthama
keyMyths.description: After the war, Ashwatthama, in a fit of rage and revenge, attacked the Pandava camp at night. Dhristadyumna was killed in his sleep, violating the rules of war and fulfilling the prophecy of his birth.

sources.author: Vyasa
sources.description: The primary source for Dhristadyumna's story and his role in the Kurukshetra War.
sources.text: The primary source for Dhristadyumna's story and his role in the Kurukshetra War.
